definition: Create a new method by extracting a block of code from an existing method to improve readability or reuse.
Replace a method call with the method's body when the method’s body is simple or no longer needed.
Change the name of a method to better reflect its purpose or improve clarity.
Relocate a method from one class to another where it fits better conceptually or logically.
Transfer an attribute from one class to another that uses it more appropriately.
Move a method from subclasses into their superclass to remove duplication.
Move a attribute from subclasses into their superclass to centralize shared state.
Move a method from a superclass to subclasses where it is more relevant.
Move an attribute from a superclass to subclasses where it is actually used.
Create a new superclass from common behavior shared among multiple classes.
Define a new interface from existing methods in one or more classes to improve abstraction.
Move a class to another package or module that better fits its responsibilities.
Change the name of a class to better describe its role or behavior.
Extract part of a method into a new one and move that new method to another class.
Change the name of a package to reflect a new structure or naming convention.
Move a class to a different package and change its name simultaneously.
Create a new class by moving related fields and methods from an existing class to simplify responsibilities.
Create a new subclass from an existing class by moving specific features or behaviors into it.
Introduce a new variable to hold the result of an expression for readability or reuse.
Replace a variable with its assigned expression when the variable adds no clarity.
Turn a constant or hard-coded value into a parameter to make code more flexible.
Create a new attribute from part of an object or data structure for clarity or reuse.
Move a method to another class and change its name for consistency or clarity.
Move a method to another class and then inline it at its call sites.
Make an attribute private and provide getter and setter methods for controlled access.
Replace a fixed attribute with one that can be set through parameters.
Relocate a package within the project structure for better organization.
Divide a large package into smaller, more cohesive ones.
Combine related packages into one to simplify structure.
Move a parameter so it is declared closer to where it is used.
Remove a redundant class hierarchy by merging subclass and superclass.
Combine two or more classes into a single class that consolidates their behavior.
Replace an attribute with its value or the expression it stores.
Divide a large class into smaller, more focused classes.
Break down complex conditional logic into separate, simpler conditions or methods.
Reverse a conditional expression and adjust its branches for clarity.
Combine multiple conditional branches that result in the same action.
Combine two or more similar methods into a single unified one.
Divide a long or complex method into smaller, more focused methods.
Relocate a block of code from one method to another to improve cohesion or reuse.
Change a variable's name to make its purpose clearer.
Change a parameter’s name for clarity or consistency.
Change the name of a class attribute for clarity or convention.
Move an attribute to another class and rename it simultaneously.
Replace a local variable with a class attribute to make the data persist across methods.
Replace one attribute with another, often during class refactoring or renaming.
Combine two or more variables with similar roles into one.
Combine multiple parameters into a single one representing a unified concept.
Combine two or more attributes with similar meaning into one.
Divide a variable that holds multiple unrelated values into separate variables.
Replace one complex parameter with multiple simpler parameters.
Replace one complex attribute with multiple more focused attributes.
Modify a variable’s data type to better fit its usage or new logic.
Change the type of a parameter to match new requirements or design.
Update a method’s return type to better reflect its output.
Alter an attribute’s data type for correctness or consistency.
Introduce a new annotation to a method for metadata or behavioral purposes.
Delete an annotation from a method that is no longer needed.
Change the parameters or type of a method annotation.
Add an annotation to a class attribute.
Remove an annotation from a class attribute.
Change the configuration or parameters of an attribute annotation.
Add an annotation to a class for metadata or framework integration.
Remove an existing annotation from a class.
Alter the parameters or type of a class annotation.
Attach an annotation to a parameter.
Remove an annotation from a parameter.
Change or update the properties of a parameter annotation.
Add an annotation to a variable for metadata or analysis.
Delete an annotation attached to a variable.
Change the annotation or its arguments for a variable.
Add a new parameter to a method signature.
Remove an unused or redundant parameter from a method.
Change the order of parameters in a method signature.
Declare that a method can throw an additional type of exception.
Remove an exception type from a method’s throws clause.
Replace an exception type in a method’s throws clause with another.
Change a method’s access level (e.g., public, private, protected).
Modify the visibility (e.g., public/private) of a class attribute.
Replace a persistent class attribute with a temporary local variable.

refactoring_type: Add Method Modifier (final, static, abstract, synchronized)
definition: Add a modifier to a method to adjust its behavior or constraints.

refactoring_type: Remove Method Modifier (final, static, abstract, synchronized)
definition: Remove a modifier from a method to change its constraints or flexibility.

refactoring_type: Add Attribute Modifier (final, static, transient, volatile)
definition: Add a modifier to an attribute to change its storage or visibility behavior.

refactoring_type: Remove Attribute Modifier (final, static, transient, volatile)
definition: Remove a modifier from an attribute to alter its behavior.
Add a modifier to a local variable to make it immutable.
Add a modifier to a parameter to prevent reassignment.
Remove the final modifier from a variable to allow reassignment.
Remove the final modifier from a parameter to allow reassignment.
Change a class’s visibility, such as from public to package-private.
Add a modifier to a class to specify inheritance or usage constraints.
Remove a modifier from a class to relax inheritance or constraints.

refactoring_type: Change Type Declaration Kind (class, interface, enum, annotation, record)
definition: Change the kind of a type declaration to another, such as from class to interface.
